name: global-coding-style
description: Write clean, consistent code following naming conventions, DRY principles, small focused functions, automated formatting, and meaningful variable names. Use this skill when writing or refactoring any code in any language, naming variables/functions/classes/files, structuring code logic, removing dead code, or ensuring code readability and maintainability. Apply to all programming tasks including Python, JavaScript, TypeScript, Ruby, Java, Go, or any other language files (.py, .js, .ts, .rb, .java, .go) to maintain consistent style, improve code quality, and enhance team collaboration across the entire codebase.
capabilities: Apply consistent naming conventions for variables, functions, and classes
Refactor code to follow DRY principles and eliminate duplication
Create small, focused functions with single responsibilities
Remove dead code, unused imports, and commented-out blocks
Format code with consistent indentation and structure
Write code optimized for readability and team collaboration
author: EIS-ITS
securitySummary: All 18 static findings are FALSE POSITIVES. This is a documentation-only skill containing markdown guidelines. The scanner misidentified: GitHub repository URLs as 'network' threats, version control hashes as 'C2 keywords', and plain English descriptions of coding concepts like 'algorithms' and 'automation' as 'cryptographic' or 'malware' patterns. No executable code exists - only documentation files.
valueStatement: Inconsistent code styles make collaboration difficult and increase maintenance costs. This skill provides AI with coding standards for writing clean, maintainable code following industry best practices. Developers use these guidelines to ensure their codebase remains readable and team members can understand each other's work.
limitations: Does not execute or run code - provides guidelines only
Does not integrate with linters or formatters automatically
Does not modify files without explicit user request
Cannot enforce style rules across a codebase without user action

useCases.title: Generate Clean Code
useCases.description: Produce well-structured code that follows established coding standards and is easy for teammates to understand

useCases.title: Enforce Style Consistency
useCases.description: Ensure submitted code follows team conventions for naming, structure, and documentation

useCases.title: Set Code Quality Standards
useCases.description: Establish consistent coding practices across multiple projects and programming languages

promptTemplates.title: Basic Code Generation
promptTemplates.prompt: Write a function that processes user input with proper naming, small focused structure, and clear documentation

promptTemplates.title: Apply DRY Principle
promptTemplates.prompt: Refactor this code to follow DRY principles, extracting repeated logic into reusable functions

promptTemplates.title: Refactor for Readability
promptTemplates.prompt: Refactor this function to improve readability with better naming, smaller functions, and cleaner structure

promptTemplates.title: Multi-Language Standards
promptTemplates.prompt: Write Python and JavaScript implementations following global coding style conventions for both languages

bestPractices: Use descriptive names that reveal intent over abbreviations
Keep functions small with one primary responsibility
Remove dead code rather than commenting it out
Apply DRY principle to eliminate duplication
antiPatterns: Using single-letter variable names except for loop counters
Creating large functions that do multiple unrelated tasks
Leaving commented-out code in the codebase
Copy-pasting code instead of extracting shared logic

faq.question: Does this skill work with all programming languages?
faq.answer: Yes, the coding principles apply to Python, JavaScript, TypeScript, Ruby, Java, Go, and most other languages.

faq.question: Can this skill modify my existing code files?
faq.answer: The skill provides guidelines but only modifies code when you explicitly request refactoring.

faq.question: Does it integrate with linters like ESLint or Black?
faq.answer: No, this skill provides AI guidance for code quality but does not run or configure external tools.

faq.question: Is my code data sent anywhere when using this skill?
faq.answer: No, this is a prompt-based skill with no network access or data transmission.

faq.question: How is this different from a code formatter?
faq.answer: Formatters handle syntax and spacing. This skill guides AI to write cleaner logic, better names, and cleaner structure.

faq.question: Can I use this with other Claude skills?
faq.answer: Yes, this skill works alongside other coding skills to ensure consistent, high-quality code output.
